pnpm_verify.note: Full gate: env:check, lint, type-check, build, test (aggregate 950 tests / 0 fail in test:ops alone, plus all app/domain/verification suites), smart-form verify, verify:commands, then test:live-db (test:db + test:t1-proof:live). All green, exit code 0.

r_level.command: pnpm exec tsx scripts/ci/r-level-check.ts --base origin/main --head HEAD
r_level.note: Re-run after rebasing onto a newer origin/main (which had picked up an unrelated ci.yml audit-step fix while this lane was running its live-DB proof suite). No R-level rule matches AGENTS.md, .claude/agents/*.md, or scripts/ci/* paths in docs/05_operations/r1-r5-rules.json.

pnpm_type_check.note: Re-run standalone after the origin/main rebase as a fast sanity check; the full pnpm verify run above (which also includes type-check) completed green before the rebase, and the rebase only picked up an unrelated ci.yml change.

queries.description: database-smoke.test.ts inserts and deletes a real submission row as part of the persist-submission-and-settlement live write-path test
database-smoke.test.ts inserts and deletes a real pick row as part of the persist-submission-and-settlement live write-path test
database-smoke.test.ts selects pick_lifecycle rows to verify no lifecycle event is written on an invalid atomic enqueue (UTV2-920 rollback test)
database-smoke.test.ts selects distribution_outbox rows to verify status/receipt/lifecycle/audit rollback on an invalid atomic delivery confirmation (UTV2-920 rollback test)
database-smoke.test.ts selects distribution_receipts rows as part of the same atomic-delivery rollback verification
database-smoke.test.ts selects audit_log rows to verify no audit row is written on an invalid atomic settlement (UTV2-920 rollback test) and that a re-settlement correction is additive (UTV2-996)
database-smoke.test.ts inserts settlement_records rows to verify a correction chain is additive and the original settlement row is not mutated (UTV2-996)

runtime_proof.row_counts_note: Live table row counts queried directly against the zfzdnfwdarxucxtaojxm Supabase project via SELECT COUNT(*) immediately after the pnpm test:db run above (which cleans up its own inserted rows via explicit deletes -- these counts reflect steady-state live data, not test artifacts). This diff's scope (agent-instruction docs + a new TypeScript CI guard script) does not itself touch any of these tables or any DB code; this runtime proof exists because T1 tier requires it unconditionally per truth-check-lib.ts's runtime_proof_required gate (tier === 'T1'), independent of whether the diff's own files touch DB code -- same posture as the prior concurrency-ramp lane's own T1 proof.
runtime_proof.test_db_tap_output:
1..7
# tests 7

status: OPEN -- PR not yet created at the time this evidence.json was written. sha_binding.merge_sha is intentionally null and will be bound automatically by post-merge-lane-close.yml's ops:proof-generate --merge-sha after merge, per the repo's standard T1 proof-SHA-binding automation.

sha_binding.merge_sha_note: PR not yet merged. Do not treat head_sha as merge truth -- GitHub main + the eventual merge commit are the authoritative source once merged. This field intentionally trails the true PR head SHA by one commit: any edit to this file to update the field is itself a new commit that advances the head SHA again. The executor-result/v1 comment's Head SHA field (validated mechanically by executor-result-validator.yml against the live PR head) is the operative binding, not this narrative field.

scope.audit_scope_note: The task also required auditing .claude/commands/dispatch.md, dispatch-board.md, lane-management.md, loop-dispatch.md, and three-brain.md for stale concurrency literals. All five were found already fully config-driven (deferring to CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json / ops:execution-state, with explicit 'do not copy numeric caps into this command' language) and required no edit -- they are read-only audit findings, not part of the diff.
historical_records_left_untouched: docs/06_status/proof/UTV2-1504/verification.md (accurate historical record of the base config at the time it was written)

scripts/ops/concurrency-simulation.test.ts (POLICY fixture intentionally uses arbitrary smaller constants to test the generic mechanism; PROD_POLICY fixture in the same file already mirrors the real 10/4/6 config)

acceptance_criteria.audit_repo_for_stale_concurrency_literal: PASS -- grep sweep for 2 Claude/4 Codex/6 lanes/2\/4\/6/etc. plus a broader active-lanes-count sweep; findings classified below
acceptance_criteria.fix_agents_md: PASS -- AGENTS.md executor-limits table and total-cap line now defer to CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json / ops:execution-state instead of embedding numbers
acceptance_criteria.fix_dispatch_md_if_stale: N/A -- already config-driven on audit, no change needed (see scope.audit_scope_note)
acceptance_criteria.fix_other_current_instruction_surfaces_f: PASS -- .claude/agents/lane-governor.md was severely stale (5-lane/2-Claude/3-Codex baseline from an even older trial) and is now fully config-driven with no embedded numbers
acceptance_criteria.preserve_historical_records: PASS -- see scope.historical_records_left_untouched; none of those files were edited
acceptance_criteria.mechanical_ci_guard_added: PASS -- scripts/ci/concurrency-doc-drift-guard.ts, narrow explicit allowlist (AGENTS.md, .claude/agents/lane-governor.md, .claude/commands/*.md), two-layer check (static stale-literal patterns + config-driven numeric-claim cross-check against live CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json base values)
acceptance_criteria.guard_wired_into_real_ci: PASS -- added to verify:static and verify:quick (both consumed by pnpm verify, which runs in .github/workflows/ci.yml) and to test:ops
acceptance_criteria.guard_detects_injected_stale_claim: PASS -- 10 dedicated tests inject each known-stale literal variant plus a config-mismatch numeric claim and assert detection
acceptance_criteria.canonical_references_accepted: PASS -- 2 dedicated tests assert CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.json references and the current correct numeric values produce zero findings
acceptance_criteria.historical_proof_paths_not_rejected: PASS -- 2 dedicated tests: the default allowlist never includes docs/06_status/proof|lanes|INCIDENTS paths, and a fixture mirroring real historical proof wording is proven to only escape detection because it is excluded from the allowlist (not because the content itself is lenient)
acceptance_criteria.config_driven_values_match_execution_sta: PASS -- 1 dedicated test asserts the guard's live_base_config equals both loadConcurrencyConfig()'s raw values and execution-state.ts's MAX_CLAUDE_LANES/MAX_CODEX_LANES (the same values pnpm ops:execution-state -- --json reports under dispatch_slots.*.max while no trial is active)
